Samus and Pac Man got to talking in the Smash Bros lobby.  

Pac Man told Samus all about his games and Samus said, “oh yeah, I’ve done some of that stuff.  Most recently, that whole thing with the EMMIs was surprisingly like that. Crazy, good times.”

Then Samus told Pac Man all about her games, and Pac Man went, “You know, all this time, and I haven’t really done that.  I should absolutely do that in my next game.”

BREAKING NEWS: Mystery of Zoo Break-In Solved.  :3

Animals deserve our respect.  They're not there for our amusement or entertainment.  Unfortunately, many haven't got that memo.

Tokyo Zoo was recently broken into.  Apparently someone had accidentally ordered the replacement of their usual security gates with impenetrable toddler gates.  During the night, Nintendo of Japan's President Shuntaro Furukawa (maturity age 2) had snuck in and released several dangerous animals from their cages.  He then tried to order them into specific spots akin to the likes of Sunderfolk.

When Zoo Security finally caught up to the fully grown president, he was hitting a perturbed polar bear in the face over and over with his plushie while shouting, "Bad bear!  Bad bear!"  

Security contemplated leaving Shuntaro Furukawa to his fate, but in the end, they knew that a dead body would cause more problems than it would fix, even the problems caused by one like Shuntaro Furukawa.  They went through great pains to pick up the grown man and run to safety as he kicked, bit, and screamed frantically.

The president managed to get a boo-boo on his knee while 20 security guards were injured in the disaster.  Needless to say, Shuntaro Furukawa is now banned from the zoo for life...

When confronted about what he was thinking, Shuntaro Furukawa simply giggled, showed us his plushie of Mossanda from Palworld, and said, "Pokemon.  Mine!"

This is probably the most bizarre yet interesting update yet

Some neat tidbits I picked up checking out Japanese reactions to this announcement:

1)  Many are complaining that they are going to lose sleep playing these games.  It appears they have the same effect as "One More Turn" in Civilization games.  They also lament that they aren't going to have enough time in the day to play through all of these.

2)  Somebody crunched some numbers and found that buying used copies of these four games would cost roughly 60,000 Yen (roughly $600.00 USD).  So these games are just as rare and collectible in Japan as they are here in English speaking regions.  There are a number of people relieved that they can now afford to play these without shelling out 10,000 Yen (at minimum) for just one of these games.

BREAKING NEWS: Latest Live Detective Game Ends in Failure :(

Live games can be a source of joy and entertainment, from scavenger hunts to escape rooms.  However, some people just can't seem to get the memo...  

As part of a publicity stunt for On Your Tail, the company behind said game created a little detective adventure for Nintendo of Japan's President Shuntaro Furukawa (maturity age 2).  The fully grown president walked through the play area, skipping around and banging his sippy cup full of orange juice at every wall.  Some clues were smashed to pieces by Shuntaro Furukawa's sledgehammer and others were actively urinated on by him.

Obviously, Shuntaro Furukawa couldn't seem to wrap his head around the fun challenges and when several five-year-olds breezed past him, he screamed and banged his fists on the groun in one of his signature temper tantrums.

Eventually, they simplified the game enough that they just handed the president the secret message on a slip of paper: Pals are not pokemon.

However, Shuntaro Furukawa screeched at the top of his lungs, held up his messy plushie of Galeclaw from Palworld, and shouted, "Pokemon!  MINE!"

Gosh the music, the aesthetic, and the 60fps really sold me. But it's amazingly huge. Over 3 gigabytes, nearing Hades' file size on Switch. On Steam, the game is no more than 2gb. And Animal Well, for example, is 85MB.


I just don't have space right now on my Switch for it, especially if it's that big and doesn't even have the full Steam game features, with USB mouse support. Handheld/tabletop would be really fun though. We'll see what happens with updates, maybe, but I don't see the game shrinking in the future if it was released like this.

Edit: I would call this game a "timing" platformer, so far. This is a very unique puzzle platformer that's more cerebral than what I'm used to in games like Celeste or Meat Boy. It's like if you pooled the memorization of VVVVV's hardest maps and put that energy into timing. For those who want a cozy game you can clear in multiple ways, there are accessbility settings, but the way the game is designed is not about moving windows around. That's just a cool part of it sometimes. :3

I’d say you can even see some Mario in this with the blue sky in the background and the green land. Yet it seems to borrow all of these ideas from decades of the best platformers and bring it into a love letter to platforming with its own twist, a lot of charm, and a punchy upbeat soundtrack to make even the grumpiest of cats meow.

BREAKING NEWS: Nintendo Employees Denied Promised Raises :(

Despite what some may think, a work relationship is not a hierarchy, but a balance.  Just as there are things and employer can reasonably expect from an employee (come to work on time, do your job, etc.), the opposite is also true (pay for work, maintain safety, have your workers' backs, etc.)  Nintendo has unfortunately broken that trust.

Inspired by a preview video of Omega 6 The Triangle Stars, Nintendo of Japan's President Shuntaro Furukawa (maturity age 2) has been on a rock-paper-scissors kick except he doesn't quite understand how the rules work.  Whenever he plays, the fully grown president instead pulls out a plushie of Gildane from Palworld and says, "Pokemon!  Mine!  I win!"

However, what Shuntaro Furukawa wouldn't acknowledge is that the International Rock-Paper-Scissors Convention of 2002 had officially ruled that if a player used anything other than rock, paper, or scissors, it was an automatic loss.  The initial reason for this was too many kids using A-Bomb gestures and the like.

Well, Shuntaro Furukawa was so confident that he promises raises to any employee who could beat him in RPS and due to an anonymous email, they all knew about the IRPSC ruling.  Needless to say, every employee claimed victory, but Shuntaro Furukawa refused to pay out.

"We signed contracts," one employee said.  "We have it in writing.  If he had played fair and competed honestly, he would've had a decent chance."

When confronted, Shuntaro Furukawa threw his sippy cup full of orange juice through the nearby window, screamed his lungs out, and banged his fists against the floor in one of his signature tantrums.  "NO!  I WON!  I DID POKEMON!  MONEY MINE!  POKEMON MINE!"

We had to run out of there fast after someone pointed out that the president's Pal plushie was not a pokemon.  As we left, a representative of Pocketpair was approaching to take advantage of a wager the president had made regarding the unjust lawsuit and RPS.  He seemed justifiably confident, especially since he had an official IRPSC referee with him...

**OFFICIAL SUBMISSION**
**Game Title:** Bus Simulator Ultimate : India 
**Console:** Nintendo Switch
**Control Mapping Submission:**
1. **Camera Views:** View buttons (-/+ or Left Stick click)
2. **Directional Control:** Left Joystick
3. **Indicators/Headlights:**
	* D-pad:
		+ Up: Hazards
		+ Down: Headlights toggle
		+ Left/Right: Indicators
4. **Transmission Control:**
	* Plus (+) button: Gear upshift
	* Minus (-) button: Gear downshift
5. **Horn:** Left Joystick click
6. **Acceleration/Braking:**
	* X button: Accelerator
	* Y button: Brake
7. **On-Screen Buttons:**
	* Cruise Control toggle (on/off)
	* Doors open/close
	* Parking brake
	* Radio/Multimedia
	* AC/Climate control
	* Windshield shades up/down
	* Windshield wipers
	* Rearview mirror adjust
	* Food/Drink service button

In this trailer, you can still see the effects of the disastrous first announcement of the Wii U and the psychological trauma it caused Nintendo. That's why in this short material we got clear information that:

- The console has 2 in its name and is the direct successor to the Switch
- A lot of "2" in the trailer so that the consumer would understand
- Mario Kart because it is their system seller after all
- Information about the compatibility of games with the Switch both in box and digitally
- Showing that it still works in portable mode and with a TV

This trailer was tailored so that even an idiot would understand that it's about the Switch 2, well done Nintendo.

The Water Buffalo Pacer test is a multistage aerobic water buffalo test that progressively gets more water buffalo as it continues. The 20 meter water buffalo test will begin in 30 seconds. Line up at the start. The water buffalo speed starts slowly, but gets faster each minute after you hear this signal *boop*. A single water buffalo should be completed each time you hear this sound *ding*. Remember to run in a straight line, and run as long as water buffalo. The second time you fail to complete a water buffalo before the sound, your test is over. The test will begin on the word water buffalo. On your mark, get ready, water buffalo.

I’m still very excited to play this game again. I don’t care that people are saying that this game came back for the third time, this game has a special place in my heart and it has been one of my favorite games on the Wii growing up. I’m one of those people that’s happy to see this game come back and I’m about to relive my childhood soon when this game comes out in January 2025. I know the game has some flaws such as the graphics looking worse and that they’re still charging full price at $60, I’ll still pick up this game at launch anyway even though I know I’m paying more money when it should’ve been $40 like the Wii version. And by the way, DK has fur again in the scene when punching the Tiki out the door. This is still my favorite game of all time and I can’t wait to play this game again after more than a decade since I last played DKC Returns. Despite people saying “Nintendo, this is the third time this game returned” I’m not upset or complaining about that since I love this game so much. I know a new DK game would be awesome, but this one is pretty good as well just like Tropical Freeze being absolute peak of a DK game. It’s been 11 years without a new DK game but I believe we’ll be getting a new one on the Switch 2. But either way, I’m thrilled to play this one again and now every single DK Country game will be available to play on Switch and this is why Returns is returning to Switch because of the theme park at Super Nintendo World. So I am very happy overall to see this game come to Switch with a pretty decent style mode and I can’t wait to play this game like the good ol’ Wii days. Thank you so much Nintendo!

*"Play Tetris, my friends."* -Alexey Pajitnov

Gotta love this man. Thank you so much for creating Tetris, Mr. Pajitnov. 😁

To think Alexey Pajitnov has been around this long to see a simple little game he made evolve into a staple of not just gaming, but pop culture as a whole for 40 years is truly something incredible. Next year, we’re hitting 40 years of Super Mario Bros. & 45 years of Pac-Man. And even further, Sonic’s turning 35 and Mega Man will be 40. Damn, it feels like yesterday when I first played Tetris…

One of the best stories regarding Tetris is when Hiroshi Yamauchi asked Shigeru Miyamoto if he thought Tetris would be better to pack-in with the Game Boy than Super Mario Land, and Miyamoto said Tetris was definitely the right choice. When Mr. Yamauchi, taken aback that Miyamoto thought Tetris was what they should go with even over Miyamoto's own character, asked Mr. Miyamoto why, he pointed out, "Have you been checking on your secretaries during their lunch breaks? They're *ALL* playing Tetris."

I remember watching The Gaming Historian's video on the history of Tetris and thinking back to those final years of the Cold War. It's absolutely crazy to see how far we've come, from those old black-and-green IBM screens, to the grey-and-green screens of the original Gameboy, to now having 99 people playing against each other all over the world to prove themselves the ultimate master of a little block-stacking game made by a bored Russian programmer in the corner of a Soviet office building.

Given everything that has happened, it's good to see Mr. Pajitnov seems to be doing well. Justice certainly prevailed in the end for him.

BREAKING NEWS: The true reason behind Nintendo's recent games being so glitchy, especially Pokemon, has been revealed.  :3

Glitches, they've always been around.  Some games are fun with them, some not so much.  Nintendo's take as of late has tended to be more of a "whiner baby" policy begging players not to trigger glitches that they can't be bothered to fix.  

However, the true cause is more problematic.  Recent footage from the Freak Leak has revealed video of when Gen 9 Pokemon was still in the beta stages.  The team was hard at work when suddenly, the current President of Nintendo of Japan, Shuntaro Furukawa (maturity age 2) barged in and screamed at the top of his lungs.

"Too slow!  Too slow!" he shouted.  "Finish it now!"  He then proceeded to toss his sippy cup at one of the computers, frying it with orange juice.  He then looked at the garbled, messed-up monitor and shouted, "Pokemon!  Mine!"  

Unfortunately, we may never know what precious data was on that computer, likely a more robust version.  When asked for comment, Shuntaro Furukawa insisted that we call him a big boy before hugging a plushie of Lamball from Palworld, pointing at it, and saying, "Pokemon!  Mine!"

BREAKING NEWS: The real reason why Fortnite is releasing Season 1 once again.  :3

Sometimes going back to basics brings a lot of nostalgia, a wish for simpler times.  Sometimes they got it right on the first try and haven't been able to replicate the magic.  Not this time, though.  Nintendo has backed Epic Games into a corner with a patent violation lawsuit.  Like with Pocketpair initially, they are refusing to divulge the specific details and now we may never know what they were.

Unlike Pocketpair, who has yet to surrender to Nintendo's blatant patent bullying, inanely vague and broad patents including concepts that are innate in practically every game to date, and monopolistic practices of pure evil, Epic Games caved and agreed to a settlement.  In exchange for letting Nintendo have full control over everything else Fortnite, Epic Games was allowed to retain Season 1 and must never innovate again.

The head of Nintendo's legal team, Biff Tannen of former Universal employ, had the following to say.  "We're being more generous than we need to be to those buttheads.  They had better learn to stay in their driveway from now on.  If they ever attempt to bite the foot that feeds them, let's just say that it's on like Godzilla."

As Epic Games attempts to pick up the pieces of what they have left, Nintendo has already ruined the rest of Fortnite with inferior hardware and terrible online capabilities.  The fanbase has largely dropped off, complaining that Fortnite was never supposed to mean 2-week load times.

To the Nintendo PR person that manages this channel, why are you removing all the comments that point the fact that some of the art is AI generated? This game isn't being published by Nintendo, so criticism is directed toward the developer, not Nintendo... But if you start going around removing perfectly valid comments just because they are pointing at the fact that some of the art in this trailer is AI generated, this starts looking like Nintendo itself (because the channel is clearly managed by or on behalf of Nintendo) is okay with AI generated content and happy to cover up for the developers by censoring valid and respectful albeit inconvenient criticism. This goes against what Furukawa said in his statement to investors about the use of AI technology. I know it looks bad, but maybe instead of censoring comments, you should be more critical of what games you advertise in the channel and not allow games that contain AI generated art? You could, I don't know, just make the developers fill in a questionnaire before accepting to advertise their games? That way you don't have to do damage control...
